summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Bookvia Password Reset feature. All 10 backend API tests passed. All frontend UI tests passed. The full password reset flow works correctly: forgot-password generates token, reset-password updates password, login with new password works, login with old password fails, token cannot be reused.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/forgot-password with existing email returns generic message - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/forgot-password with non-existent email returns same generic message (security) - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/forgot-password with empty email returns 400 - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/forgot-password without email field returns 400 - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/reset-password with invalid token returns 400 - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/reset-password with password < 6 chars returns 400 - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/reset-password without token returns 400 - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/reset-password without password returns 400 - PASSED
Backend: POST /api/auth/business/login still works for business owner - PASSED
Backend: Full reset flow (forgot → get token → reset → login new → login old fails → token reuse fails) - PASSED
Frontend: /forgot-password shows form with email input and 'Enviar instrucciones' button - PASSED
Frontend: /forgot-password after submit shows '¡Correo enviado!' with email and options - PASSED
Frontend: /reset-password without token shows 'Enlace inválido' with 'Solicitar nuevo enlace' button - PASSED
Frontend: /reset-password?token=VALID shows password form with confirmation - PASSED
Frontend: Password validation - mismatch shows red error, match shows green success - PASSED
Frontend: /reset-password after successful reset shows '¡Contraseña actualizada!' with 'Iniciar sesión' button - PASSED
Frontend: /login has '¿Olvidaste tu contraseña?' link pointing to /forgot-password - PASSED
Frontend: Business login (testrealstripe@bookvia.com / Test1234!) still works - PASSED
critical_code_review_comments: forgot-password endpoint correctly returns generic message for both existing and non-existing emails (security best practice)
reset-password endpoint validates token, password length (min 6 chars), and removes token after use
Password reset also updates password_hash in businesses collection if user is a business owner
Token expiration is set to 1 hour (password_reset_expires field)
Email is sent from noreply@bookvia.app (NOREPLY_EMAIL constant)
Frontend ForgotPasswordPage has proper data-testid attributes for testing
Frontend ResetPasswordPage has real-time password match validation (red/green feedback)
Frontend handles both token present and token missing states correctly
API endpoints are properly integrated in frontend api.js (authAPI.forgotPassword, authAPI.resetPassword)

test_user_for_reset.note: Password was changed during previous testing

seed_data_creation: Test users created during testing with prefix 'testreset_e2e_' were cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Password reset feature is fully implemented and tested. Key flows: (1) POST /api/auth/forgot-password generates password_reset_token and password_reset_expires (1 hour), (2) POST /api/auth/reset-password validates token, updates password_hash, removes token, (3) Email is MOCKED in development (stored in sent_emails collection). Frontend pages: /forgot-password → /reset-password?token=XXX → /login. The email template uses NOREPLY_EMAIL (noreply@bookvia.app) and includes a styled button linking to the reset page.

mocked_apis.email_service: Email sending is configured with Resend (RESEND_API_KEY is set) but in development mode emails are also stored in sent_emails collection for debugging
